text.tspan: EVENT SOURCING: store every state change as an immutable EVENT.
Current state is computed by replaying events.
The event log IS the database.
'what would the balance be if we ignored Jan 16 fraud?'
u2022 COMPLETE HISTORY: every change preserved
u2022 TIME TRAVEL: 'what was the state on date X?'
u2022 AUDIT BUILT-IN: log IS the audit trail
u2022 NEW PROJECTIONS: add a new view; replay events to populate it
u2022 NATURAL FIT for CQRS, event-driven architecture, [outbox](../data/outbox.md)
u2022 DOMAIN INSIGHT: events name what happened (DDD-friendly)
u2022 BUG FIX BY REWRITE: discovered a bug; rewrite projection; replay events
u2022 querying current state requires replay or maintained projection (eventual consistency)
u2022 schema evolution of events is HARD (events are immutable, but their interpretation evolves)
u2022 event volume grows linearly forever (u2192 snapshots, archival)
u2022 mental model shift: not 'rows that change' but 'facts that accumulate'
u2022 NOT every system benefits: simple CRUD apps just want UPDATE
... never overwritten; new events appended forever ...
CURRENT BALANCE: replay all events u2192 u03a3 deposits u2212 u03a3 withdrawals = 1500
Each projection can be rebuilt by replaying events.
